scheme_name: Scholarship/Merit Scholarship Schools SC/ST/OBC/Min (Studying in College/Technical/ Professional Institutions)
page_title: Scholarship/Merit Scholarship Schools SC/ST/OBC/Min (Studying in College/Technical/ Professional Institutions)

details: Launched by the Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC, Government of NCT of Delhi, the scheme "Merit Scholarship to SC/ST/OBC/Minority students studying in recognised Colleges/Professional/Technical Institutions" aims to provide financial assistance to students from marginalized communities pursuing higher education in recognized institutions. The scheme offers monthly scholarships to hostellers and day scholars based on their course of study, with amounts ranging from ₹420/- to ₹1,860/- per month. Eligible students must have secured at least 60% marks in the previous academic year. Family income limits apply only to OBC and Minority students. The scheme is implemented by the Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC, Government of NCT of Delhi.
benefits: Degree courses in medical/engineering/B.Sc. (Agri)/Veterinary Science/Forensic Science/Higher Technical and professional studies, and degree-level courses in Indian medicine (B.A.M.S. and comparable courses in Ayurvedic, Unani/Tibbia, and Homeopathy systems of Medicine): Hostellers: ₹1,620/- per month for 12 months; Day Scholars: ₹900/- per month for 12 months.
For Postgraduate courses in the above fields and other technical/professional courses: Hostellers: ₹1,860/- per month for 12 months; Day Scholars: ₹960/- per month for 12 months.

Diploma-level courses in Indian medicine, engineering, technology, architecture, and other vocational courses, as well as postgraduate courses in science subjects: Hostellers: ₹1,110/- per month for 12 months; Day Scholars: ₹720/- per month for 12 months.

Certificate courses in engineering, technology, architecture, medicine, and other vocational courses (minimum duration of one year): Hostellers: ₹930/- per month for 12 months; Day Scholars: ₹630/- per month for 12 months.
General courses up to graduate level: Hostellers: ₹804/- per month for 12 months; Day Scholars: ₹420/- per month for 12 months.
Postgraduate studies in Arts, Commerce, and other subjects: Hostellers: ₹930/- per month for 12 months; Day Scholars: ₹630/- per month for 12 months.
The scholarship amount will be reduced to 50% if a student fails the annual examination but continues studies, except in cases of medical grounds or natural calamities.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
The applicant should belong to the SC/ST/OBC/Minority category.
The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized school, college, or institution in India.
The applicant should be a student.
The applicant should have secured a 60% or above marks in the previous examination.
If the applicant is from OBC/Minority Community, his/her annual family income should not exceed ₹2,00,000/-.

online: of the Govt. of NCT of Delhi.
On the top right corner of the homepage, click “
". You will be taken to the "Citizen Registration Form". Carefully read the “Guidelines for Online Registration” provided at the bottom of the page.
In the Citizen Registration Form, select "Aadhaar Card" as the Document Type, and provide the Aadhaar Number. Fill in the captcha code and click “Continue”.

On the next page, fill in all the mandatory fields. If required, verify your Email ID and Mobile Number via OTP. Enter the Captcha code and click “Continue to Register”. You will receive your Login ID and Password on the Mobile Number/Email Address provided during the registration.
”, you will be taken to the “Citizen Login Form”
Provide your Login ID and Password, enter the Captcha code and click “Login”.
Go to the home page and click “Apply Service”. Navigate to the scheme that you intend to apply for. Click on the name of the scheme. You will be taken to the scheme's online application form.

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions and/or declaration (if any), then click "Finish”.
An OTP will be received on the registered mobile number. Provide the OTP and click “Submit”. An acknowledgement of successful submission shall be sent over Email/SMS/Both.
Fill in all the mandatory fields. Enter the Captcha Code, and click "Submit".
Your current application status will be displayed.

documents_required: Income Certificate issued by competent authorities of GNCTD (for OBC and Minority students).
Affidavit, in the case of a gap in education up to 3 years.
Caste Certificate issued by SDM/Deputy Commissioner (Revenue), GNCTD, in the name of the student or father.
Domicile Certificate of Delhi (if the caste certificate is not issued by GNCTD).
Proof of residency in Delhi for the past three years (e.g., EPIC card, Aadhaar Number, Ration Card, MTNL landline bill, Driving License, or Domicile Certificate).
Declaration by parent/guardian affirming the student's minority community status.

faqs.question: What is the monthly scholarship amount for hostellers pursuing degree courses in medical or engineering under Group-A?
faqs.answer: The monthly scholarship for hostellers in Group-A degree courses like medical or engineering is ₹1,620 for 12 months.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for day scholars studying diploma-level courses in engineering or technology under Group-B?
faqs.answer: Day scholars in Group-B diploma-level courses like engineering or technology receive ₹720 per month for 12 months.

faqs.question: Are students pursuing postgraduate courses in science subjects eligible for financial assistance under Group-B?
faqs.answer: Yes, postgraduate students in science subjects under Group-B are eligible for ₹1,110 (hostellers) or ₹720 (day scholars) monthly for 12 months.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for hostellers enrolled in certificate courses under Group-C?
faqs.answer: Hostellers in Group-C certificate courses receive ₹930 per month for 12 months.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided to day scholars pursuing general graduate-level courses under Group-D?
faqs.answer: Day scholars in Group-D general graduate-level courses get ₹420 per month for 12 months.

faqs.question: Is there an income limit for SC/ST students applying for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, there is no income limit for SC/ST students, but OBC and minority students must have a family income below ₹2,00,000 per annum.

faqs.question: What documents are required to prove minority community residency in Delhi?
faqs.answer: Proof includes EPIC card, Aadhaar, ration card, MTNL landline bill, driving license, domicile certificate, or a nationalized bank passbook.

faqs.question: What is the minimum academic requirement for students to qualify for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students must have scored at least 60% marks in the previous academic year to be eligible.

faqs.question: Can students receiving stipends from other institutions or the government still apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, students receiving stipends from other sources are still eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: What happens if a student fails their annual examination but continues their studies?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount is reduced to 50% unless the failure is due to medical reasons or circumstances beyond their control.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for hostellers pursuing postgraduate courses in arts or commerce under Group-D?
faqs.answer: Hostellers in Group-D postgraduate arts or commerce courses receive ₹930 per month for 12 months.

faqs.question: Are diploma courses in hotel management or catering covered under Group-B?
faqs.answer: Yes, diploma courses in hotel management or catering under Group-B are eligible for ₹1,110 (hostellers) or ₹720 (day scholars) monthly.

faqs.question: What is the financial assistance for day scholars in postgraduate technical or professional courses under Group-A?
faqs.answer: Day scholars in Group-A postgraduate technical or professional courses receive ₹960 per month for 12 months.

faqs.question: Is there a residency requirement for minority community students applying for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, minority students must be Delhi residents for the past three years and provide valid proof of residency.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for hostellers pursuing diploma-level courses in Indian medicine like BAMS?
faqs.answer: Hostellers in Group-B diploma-level Indian medicine courses like BAMS receive ₹1,110 per month for 12 months.

faqs.question: Are certificate courses in agriculture or dairy covered under Group-C?
faqs.answer: Yes, certificate courses in agriculture or dairy under Group-C are eligible for ₹930 (hostellers) or ₹630 (day scholars) monthly.
